key_insight: PA-SciML adds a verification-first, physics-audited workflow so agentic SciML models are accepted only when their predicted fields satisfy explicit mechanics checks, not just low error.
engineering_relevance: This helps computational solid mechanics teams trust surrogate models for design and simulation because the workflow can catch boundary-condition, scaling, and causality violations that standard error metrics miss.
method: Fix the evaluator before search and derive machine-checkable physics requirements.
Test each candidate surrogate directly on outputs and search for high-violation cases over prescribed input or load-history ranges.
Optionally use advisory numerical probes before training and isolate one modeling change at a time to track which edits improve score.
evidence_signal: In the reported examples, the static elasticity surrogate beat the error-only baseline on validation error while both passed linear-elastic checks, and in transient elastodynamics an error-only baseline with similar mean error failed a causality check whereas the selected surrogate passed.
watch_item: Verification quality depends on how complete and stringent the chosen physics checks and violation searches are, so weak or incomplete audits could still miss important failure modes.
paper_title: Physics-Audited Agentic Discovery in Scientific Machine Learning
